title: Simplex Consensus: A Simple and Fast Consensus Protocol
quote: a new and simple consensus protocol in the partially synchronous setting, tolerating f < n/3 byzantine faults, which is essentially as simple to describe as the simplest known protocols, but it also enjoys an even simpler security proof, while matching and even improving the efficiency of the state-of-the-art

title: Simplex Consensus: A Simple and Fast Consensus Protocol
Simplex Consensus | State-of-the-Art Consensus Protocol
quote: Worst-case scenario (1/3 proposers faulty, 80ms message delay): Simplex 400 ms, Algorand Agreement 480 ms, HotStuff 2480 ms, Tendermint (Chained) 1840 ms. Production implementations: Commonware (Rust), Tempo, Solana Alpenglow (Votor variant), and Ava Labs (Go).

title: Simplex Consensus — Protocol in a Nutshell
quote: Players execute iterations h = 1, 2, 3, ... For each iteration h, its leader Lₕ is chosen by hashing the iteration number h: Lₕ = H*(h) mod n. A 'notarization' for a block b is a set of signed messages from 2n/3 unique players. If player i sees 2n/3 ⟨finalize, h⟩ messages... it deems iteration h finalized. Player i sends a vote for the dummy block if its timer Tₕ fires after 3Δ seconds. Fault threshold: f < n/3.

title: commonware_consensus::simplex — module documentation
quote: All logic is split into four components: the Batcher, the Voter, the Resolver, and the Application. Vote types: notarize(c,v), nullify(v), finalize(c,v). Certificates assembled when 2f+1 votes of a given type collected. Pluggable schemes: ed25519, bls12381_multisig, secp256r1, bls12381_threshold. Divergences from the paper: fetch missing certs on demand; distinct nullify message; leader timeout for unresponsive leaders; skip leader timeout after r inactive views; message rebroadcast; treat local proposal/verification failure as immediate timeout; require application certify before broadcasting finalize.

title: commonwarexyz/monorepo — consensus/src/simplex/mod.rs (module doc)
quote: Wicked Fast Block Times (2 Network Hops). Optimal Finalization Latency (3 Network Hops). Externalized Uptime and Fault Proofs. Require Certification Before Finalization. Operates through four components: the Batcher, the Voter, the Resolver, and the Application. After a payload is notarized, the application can optionally delay or prevent finalization.

title: commonwarexyz/monorepo — simplex/types.rs (vote and certificate types)
quote: Vote enum: Notarize, Nullify, Finalize. Certificate enum: Notarization, Nullification, Finalization. Artifact enum aggregates votes and certificates plus Certification(Round, bool) for locally certified notarizations. VoteTracker manages per-signer votes via separate AttributableMap instances.

title: commonwarexyz/monorepo — simplex/engine.rs (engine wiring)
quote: Three actor design: Voter (manages vote aggregation and consensus state), Batcher (performs batch signature verification before forwarding valid votes), Resolver (request-response certificate fetching used when a node needs to catch up). Three network channels: vote_network, certificate_network, resolver_network. Configuration: epoch, leader_timeout, certification_timeout, activity_timeout, skip_timeout, timeout_retry, mailbox_size, fetch_concurrent, fetch_timeout.

title: Commonware: the Anti-Framework
quote: consensus::simplex is refreshingly minimal. It delivers simple agreement over opaque 'blobs'. Generate a new block after 2 network hops...and finalize a block after 3 network hops, theoretically yielding ~300ms block times and ~450ms finality with average network latency between participants of ~150ms.

title: Many-to-Many Interoperability with Threshold Simplex
quote: consensus::threshold_simplex employs threshold cryptography (specifically BLS12-381 threshold signatures with a 2f+1 of 3f+1 quorum) to generate both a bias-resistant beacon (for leader election and post-facto execution randomness) and succinct consensus certificates (verifiable with just the static public key) for each view with zero message overhead. Threshold signatures packaged into ~240-byte certificates. Block election is driven by the embedded VRF (the leader of view v+1 is only known at the conclusion of view v).

title: commonwarexyz/monorepo — README and stability matrix
quote: 5-level stability framework: ALPHA (breaking changes expected, no migration), BETA (formats stable, breaking changes have migration), GAMMA (API stable, extensively tested and fuzzed), DELTA (battle-tested, bug-bounty eligible), EPSILON (feature-frozen). Latest release v2026.4.0 (April 14, 2026). Primitives are designed for deployment in adversarial environments. Examples may include insecure code... not intended to be used directly in production.

title: Alpenglow: A New Consensus for Solana
quote: Votor is a new voting mechanism that replaces Tower BFT, Proof of History, and gossip for vote propagation. Simplex demonstrates efficient Byzantine agreement can be reached in a rotating leader Proof-of-Stake setting with extremely small messages, provided there is a tight upper-bound on network delay. Building on these insights, Votor achieves consensus in one or two rounds.

title: Alpenglow: Solana's Great Consensus Rewrite
quote: Alpenglow promises to dramatically lower transaction finality times from over ten seconds to the low-hundreds of milliseconds, replacing Tower BFT with the Votor voting mechanism inspired by Simplex Consensus.

title: What are the main innovations of commonware? — Discussion #240
quote: reliable open source software for building your own blockchain that incorporates state-of-the-art techniques and is easy to mod. Modular primitives where developers can swap core components without forking the entire stack.

title: commonware-consensus on crates.io
quote: commonware-consensus is ALPHA software and is not yet recommended for production use.

title: Sing a Song of Simplex
quote: fleshes out details of the Simplex atomic broadcast protocol and modifies it so that leaders disperse blocks in a more communication-efficient fashion, resulting in a protocol called DispersedSimplex that maintains the simplicity and optimal latency characteristics of the original Simplex protocol. Includes a variant that supports stable leaders.

title: From Tendermint to Simplex
quote: Simplex parties move to the next view upon receiving a cert in this view... Tendermint view-change timeout 6Δ vs Simplex 3Δ. The 2Δ wait is removed because higher certificates no longer break liveness. Each faulty leader can waste less time.

title: Reducing Block Time (and Resource Usage) with Buffered Signatures
quote: Alto, a minimal (and wicked fast) blockchain for continuously benchmarking the Commonware Library: block time 255ms → 200ms (20% reduction), finality 375ms → 300ms (20% reduction), CPU 26% → 9% on a c7g.xlarge (65% reduction) after implementing buffered signature verification.
